prerequisites: Check available disk space: dir bootflash: (NX-OS/IOS-XE) or show media (IOS-XR)
Backup running configuration to external server
Verify new image integrity (MD5/SHA512 checksum against cisco.com)
Review release notes for target version — check for known caveats and ISSU support
Confirm hardware compatibility (image must support installed line cards / supervisors)
Schedule maintenance window with change control approval
Ensure console access is available (not just SSH/VTY)
For dual-sup systems: verify standby sup is online and in sync
purpose: Upgrade, downgrade, or patch the OS image on a network device [iosxr — method: install package model (add/activate/commit); platforms: Cisco 8000, NCS 5500, NCS 540/560, ASR 9000, XRv 9000]

rollback.action: install rollback to <committed-point> — rolls back to a previous committed state
If not committed: reload will auto-rollback to previous committed packages
For emergency: 'install rollback to last-committed' from admin mode

steps.action: Copy image to harddisk or repository
steps.notes: IOS-XR uses ISO images or individual packages. GISO (Golden ISO) bundles base + SMUs + config for single-step install.
Extracts and stages packages. Monitor with 'show install request'.
Activates the packages. Depending on platform and package type, this may require a reload or be hitless (for SMUs). Use 'install activate id <op-id>' to reference the add operation.
CRITICAL: Without commit, the system rolls back to the previous version on next reload. Always commit after validation.

verification.look_for: Target version packages listed as Active

verification.confirms_if: Active packages are committed (persist across reload)
All line cards and RSPs are operational
verification.look_for: All nodes show IOS XR RUN state

warnings: ISSU is NOT supported on all platforms or between all version combinations — always verify the ISSU compatibility matrix
Always have console access before starting an upgrade — if the device fails to boot you need console to recover
Verify boot variables after upgrade to prevent boot loop on next reload
On dual-sup systems, upgrade BOTH supervisors — mismatched versions cause unpredictable behavior
SMU/GISO patches may have dependencies — install in the order specified by release notes
Never delete the currently running image until the new image is confirmed stable
IOS-XR install operations run from admin mode on some older platforms (admin install add ...)
Cisco 8000 with 24.x: GISO is the recommended upgrade method — bundles all packages and SMUs
Parallel reload: IOS-XR can reload line cards in parallel to reduce downtime — verify with 'install activate ... noprompt parallel-reload'
SMU install can be hitless (process restart) or requires LC reload — check SMU release notes
